select_when: Familiar frames are producing stale options because hidden assumptions remain unchallenged.
The blockage is conceptual rather than effort-based, so more analysis inside the current frame is unlikely to help.
Divergence should come before convergence so the team can widen the set of possible options first.
A problem needs reframing or deliberate interruption of the dominant mental template before commitment.
avoid_when: Avoiding fixation is being used as an excuse to reject all prior knowledge instead of separating useful pattern recognition from stale lock-in.
The team is generating novelty without any path back to execution or selection.

curation_notes.summary: Focused on hidden-assumption disruption and option-space widening rather than on generic creativity rhetoric.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor innovation-process sprawl because the raw source is already strongest on reframing and divergence-before-convergence.
open_questions: Execution is source-real as a limitation, but the operational file stays on opening better option spaces.

select_when.note: The raw source is explicit on stale frames, conceptual blockage, and divergence-before-convergence.

avoid_when.note: The anti-overcorrection warning is reviewed normalization of the raw anti-pattern material.

input_type.note: The raw source describes stale frames and blocked idea spaces; the compact label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The output label compresses reframing into a reusable intervention type.

reasoning_types.note: The model relies on analogical shifts and reflective interruption of current frames.
